title: Field Briefing: URLs, Destinations, and Link Purpose
summary: Field Briefing: URLs, Destinations, and Link Purpose uses an original link destination briefing scenario to retrieve earlier HTML skills, add a bounded artifact layer, and verify transfer with source and browser evidence.
objectives: Retrieve html links urls without step-by-step imitation.
Produce and verify a real link destination briefing artifact or evidence report.
Preserve earlier valid requirements while diagnosing changed content and constraints.

steps.why: Field Briefing: URLs, Destinations, and Link Purpose requires this decision because each decision must preserve document meaning, user control, portability, and inspectable evidence. This is checkpoint 1 in the link destination briefing.

options.text: Link destination briefing case 1: every internal project link needs the author’s full local file-system path.
Link destination briefing case 1: the href must use the right URL form and the link text should identify the destination or action.
Link destination briefing case 1: a fragment link targets a heading by its visible text without requiring an id.

content.text: Reconstruct the link destination briefing sequence by dependency, not by how familiar each term looks.

options.text: link destination briefing phase 2.1: identify the destination and user intent
link destination briefing phase 2.2: choose absolute, relative, fragment, email, or telephone URL syntax
link destination briefing phase 2.3: write link text that names the destination or action
link destination briefing phase 2.4: activate the link and verify the resulting location

steps.title: Read the link destination briefing contract · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which practice follows the explanation and remains valid in a changed document? Work context: link destination briefing.
steps.why: Field Briefing: URLs, Destinations, and Link Purpose requires this decision because each decision must preserve document meaning, user control, portability, and inspectable evidence. This is checkpoint 4 in the link destination briefing.

content.text: link destination briefing: A link combines an understandable accessible name with a URL. Absolute URLs identify complete locations; relative URLs preserve project relationships; fragment URLs target IDs in a document; and mailto or tel schemes offer appropriate communication actions. Link purpose must remain clear outside its surrounding sentence.

hints: Field Briefing: URLs, Destinations, and Link Purpose: first isolate the evidence required by “Read the link destination briefing contract.”
